summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for showing drive letters first. Microsoft Open Specifications documents showDriveLetter under Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst with 1 = enable and 0 = disable, the 25H2 raw registry dump lists the same value under both machine and default-user Explorer branches, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst with both Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Show Drive Letters First
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can show drive letters ahead of the drive name, or leave that presentation disabled.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this when they prefer seeing drive letters more prominently in Explorer, or when they want the simpler default presentation without drive letters shown first.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it makes drive letters more prominent, while disabling it leaves Explorer on the simpler presentation path.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the showDriveLetter folder option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowDriveLettersFirst under both the machine Explorer branch and the default-user Explorer branch, which corroborates that the value family still exists on current builds. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowDriveLettersFirst = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowDriveLettersFirst = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1.

allowed_values.label: Do not show drive letters first
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not enable the drive-letter-first presentation. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Show drive letters first
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer enables the drive-letter-first presentation. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented 0/1 semantics against the live Explorer runtime surface on Win25H2Clean.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes ShowDriveLettersFirst through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer value that the runtime probe validated.

recommended_profiles.label: Show drive letters first
intended_for: Users who want drive letters shown more prominently in Explorer
avoid_for: Users who prefer the simpler default presentation

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to show drive letters more prominently.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer not to emphasize drive letters in the Explorer UI.
tradeoffs: Makes drive letters more visible in Explorer.
Changes the default drive presentation style.

recommended_profiles.label: Do not show drive letters first
intended_for: Users who prefer the simpler Explorer presentation
avoid_for: Users who want drive letters emphasized

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to avoid the drive-letter-first presentation.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on prominently visible drive letters.
tradeoffs: Keeps Explorer on the non-drive-letter-first presentation.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista showDriveLetter
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps showDriveLetter to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable. The description says the option displays drive letters.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry corroboration for ShowDriveLettersFirst
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowDriveLettersFirst under the machine Explorer branch and under the default-user Explorer branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowDriveLettersFirst runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowDriveLettersFirst = 0 and ShowDriveLettersFirst = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the 0/1 semantics, the 25H2 raw registry dump corroborates the value family on current builds, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states on this build. This is a low-risk user-scope Explorer preference.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\ShowDriveLettersFirst ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=False
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. Baseline absence was restored after the probe.
